articles.title: Call Of Duty Movie Director Said War Games Are Pathetic And For "Weak" People
articles.description: Peter Berg is signed on to direct the upcoming Call of Duty movie from writer Taylor Sheridan. It's now come to light that Berg said in 2013 that video games are "pathetic" and people should go outside and do something instead of playing games for hours on end. He also shared some words for Call of Duty specifically, saying the only people who play the game that he gives a pass to around soldiers who play the game for entertainment while deployed. GamesRadar did the digging and found that these quotes from an Esquire interview in 2013 when Berg promoting his war movie Lone Survivor. Berg, being a public advocate for "American manhood," as the interview put it, was asked for his take on video games. "Pathetic. Pathetic. Keyboard courage. Can't stand it. The only people that I give a Call of Duty get-out-of-jail-free card to is the military. They're out there serving and they're bored and they want to entertain themselves? Okay, maybe. Kids? Uh-uh," he said. Berg, who spent time with real Navy SEALs for the Lone Survivor movie, including the man the film was based on--Marcus Luttrell--said he knows some SEALs play video games. But he tells these people that he thinks doing so is "pathetic." "I think anyone that sits around playing video games for four hours ... It's weak. Get out, do something," he said. Berg said these things more than a decade ago, and his opinions may have changed since then. It's also possible they have not, nor can anyone say if his opinions on games would have any bearing on the Call of Duty movie. The Call of Duty movie is set for release in June 2028 . It's coming from movie studio Paramount, which is run by Call of Duty super-fan David Ellison . So far, there is no word yet on a cast for the film. Activision has been trying to make a Call of Duty movie for a decade already , and there are never any guarantees in Hollywood. Paramount is also in the process of trying to buy Warner Bros. Discovery for over $100 billion . The Call of Duty vs. Battlefield rivalry will extend to the film space, it appears, as a Battlefield movie is also in the works and could star Michael B. Jordan .

articles.title: 10 delicious Indian sweets made with fresh cheese
articles.description: The delicious taste of fresh cottage cheese (chhena) is part of what makes Indian desserts so special. While Western-made cheeses are mostly made to be enjoyed in savoury dishes, Indian desserts are where the delight of using fresh, homemade cheese is at its best. This wonderful product is made by straining curdled milk and kneading it into a soft dough for many of the delicious, sweet dishes of India.From the soft sponge-like rasgulla and soaking it in syrup to the caramelised and rustic chhena poda from Odisha, both are popular and connected with tradition and celebration. Regardless of your preference for light and delicate versus rich and decadent, the list of sweets made from chhena is endless.

articles.title: In 2024, an Australian farmer clearing scrub followed a strange metallic glint to a prehistoric world hidden for 15 million years

This rare find at McGrath's Flat offers scientists an unprecedented glimpse into a prehistoric world of giant insects and ancient flora, revealing soft-bodied details usually lost to time. The discovery highlights the hidden historical treasures within seemingly ordinary landscapes.

articles.title: Cut Mangoes Like a Pro: The Easy Glass Trick Everyone Is Talking About
articles.description:
Mango season is here, and with it comes the joy of enjoying this sweet, juicy fruit in every home. But while eating mangoes is fun, cutting and peeling them can often be messy and time-consuming. Sticky hands, uneven cuts, and wasted pulp are common problems many people face. That’s why a simple and smart mango-cutting trick is going viral on social media. This easy “glass hack” helps you remove the pulp quickly without wasting any fruit, making your mango experience smoother, cleaner, and much more enjoyable this summer.

Why This Mango Hack Is Going Viral

This mango peeling hack is becoming popular because it requires very little effort and almost no knife work. The biggest advantage is that no pulp is wasted, which is usually a common issue while cutting mangoes. People often like to serve mangoes neatly on a plate, but cutting them perfectly is not as easy as it looks. Even a small mistake can ruin the shape and leave pulp stuck to the peel. This new trick solves that problem completely.

ALSO READ | Harbhajan Singh’s Rs 7 crore Bungalow: Bhajji’s Home In Jalandhar Has A Theatre And Cricket Room

The Viral ‘Glass Hack’ Everyone Is Talking About

This trick is trending on social media because it is simple and effective. You only need a regular glass—no special tools required. With this method, the mango pulp separates cleanly from the peel in one go.

View this post on Instagram

A post shared by Sonia Marzi (@fareandfork)

How To Prepare The Mango

Before trying the hack, follow these basic steps:

  • Wash the mango properly.

  • Cut it into two large pieces using a knife.

  • Remove the seed (pit) from the middle.

Now you have two thick mango slices ready to use.

ALSO READ | 8 Breezy Beachwear Looks By Kriti Sanon That Define Easy Summer Style

Step-By-Step: How To Use The Glass Trick

  • Take a clean glass (steel or glass).

  • Hold the mango slice in your hand.

  • Place the edge of the glass between the pulp and the peel.

  • Gently press and slide the glass downward.

As you do this, the entire pulp will come off smoothly and fall into the glass in one piece.

Benefits Of This Simple Trick

  • No mango pulp is wasted

  • Saves time and effort

  • Keeps your hands less sticky

  • Gives perfectly shaped mango pieces

After removing the pulp, you can cut it into smaller pieces and serve it however you like.

Enjoy Mangoes Without The Hassle

This easy hack not only saves time but also makes eating mangoes more fun and convenient. With less effort and zero waste, you can enjoy every bite of your favourite summer fruit.

articles.title: ‘Patriot Is Made For Malayalis, Will Cater To People Of Kerala,’ Says Mammoothy At Press Meet In Kochi
articles.description:
During a press meet for Patriot in Kochi, actor Mammootty said something that has created a chatter on social media platforms. The veteran star said that the film is primarily made for Malayali viewers and is not intended as a pan-India project. The film boasts a star-studded cast, including Mohanlal, Fahadh Faasil, Kunchacko Boban, Revathy, and Nayanthara, and is directed by Mahesh Narayanan. It is produced by Anto Joseph and Anil Kumar.

‘Film Is Made For Malayalis’

“This film is made for Malayalis. While we hope it may eventually find an audience outside Kerala, the primary goal was to cater to the people of Kerala, whose cinematic tastes have evolved significantly over the years,” Mammootty said. He further clarified, “This is not a pan-India film.”

ALSO READ| Jackky Bhagnani Holds His Ears To Say Sorry To Rakul Preet Singh For ‘Situationship’ Remark: WATCH

Despite this, the actor expressed confidence that Patriot could be a landmark for the Malayalam industry. “Patriot will be a game-changer. It will set a new standard and hopefully open the doors for more big-budget films in Malayalam cinema,” he added.

This is not it. One of the biggest highlights of the film is the much-anticipated face-off between Mammootty and Mohanlal. The two legendary actors will share screen space after a gap of 13 years. Speaking about their confrontation scenes, Mammootty said they are not included merely for spectacle.

“These fights are not included just for spectacle. There’s a deeper reason behind them, which will become clear once the audience sees the film,” he explained.

ALSO READ| Aamir Khan Tears Up At Son Junaid Khan’s ‘Ek Din’ Event, Calls Sai Pallavi ‘Best Actress’

About Patriot

In the spy-action film Patriot, Mammootty portrays a retired JAG officer whose life is upended by a wrongful accusation of espionage. Forced to fight for his reputation, he is drawn into a high-stakes undercover operation involving the country’s safety.

The plot thickens with the arrival of a veteran military specialist, played by Mohanlal, who partners with him to navigate the mission’s complexities. However, the team's cohesion is tested by Fahadh Faasil’s character, an official whose deep-seated skepticism about their partnership suggests hidden dangers and a potential betrayal within the ranks.

The film releases on May 1.

articles.title: Aamir Khan and girlfriend Gauri Spratt turn Junaid Khan and Sai Pallavi's 'Ek Din' event into family affair; star couple attends event with their sons - WATCH
articles.description: Aamir Khan graced a special screening of his son Junaid's film 'Ek Din', accompanied by his younger son Azad and girlfriend Gauri Spratt. Aamir, visibly emotional during the viewing, lauded co-star Sai Pallavi as the "best actress we have in our country today" and praised Junaid's efforts.

articles.title: ‘Dhurandhar 2’ box office collection day 39: Ranveer Singh’s film continues to soar; mints Rs 3.40 cr amid ‘Michael,’ ‘Bhooth Bangla’ buzz
articles.description: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' continues its phenomenal run, crossing Rs 1130 crore in India and nearing Rs 1780 crore globally after 39 days. The film demonstrated remarkable growth over its sixth weekend, outperforming competition. Despite new releases, its enduring popularity is evident in its sustained box office success.

articles.title: New mom Hailee Steinfeld gives rare glimpse into family life with emotional note about her brother
articles.description: Hailee Steinfeld’s latest newsletter offers a rare, personal look at her relationship with her brother Griffin, blending family insight with conversations about ambition and discipline. Griffin discusses launching his business and staying true to his vision, while both highlight the importance of communication in their bond. The feature also adds a lighter touch as Griffin shares his excitement about becoming an uncle, giving readers a more intimate view of Steinfeld’s life beyond public headlines.

articles.title: Dialogue of the day from Sholay: 'Kitne aadmi the?'
articles.description: Dialogue of the day: This 1975 classic action-adventure film is directed by Ramesh Sippy, bankrolled by his father GP Sippy, and written by Salim–Javed.

articles.title: For Raghu Rai, no story was too small
articles.description: His portraits reflect his sustained engagement with his subjects, from Mother Teresa to the Dalai Lama, from Satyajit Ray to Bhimsen Joshi, from Himmat Shah to Arundhati Roy

articles.title: 'Leader' OTT release: Legend Saravanan film to stream on THIS platform - Reports
articles.description: Legend Saravanan's second film, 'Leader,' a Tamil spy thriller, has garnered mixed reviews and a modest box office performance. The movie, which saw Saravanan return as actor and producer, is reportedly heading for an OTT release on Prime Video around May 1, though an official announcement is pending.

articles.title: Laughter Chefs 3: Sunita Ahuja breaks down in tears as Kashmera Shah holds her feet and apologises; watch
articles.description: The long-standing feud between Sunita Ahuja, wife of the famous actor Govinda, and the comedy duo Krushna Abhishek and Kashmera Shah has seemingly come to a heartfelt conclusion on the set of Laughter Chefs Season 3. In tear-jerking promos, viewers witness Sunita extending her forgiveness to the couple, while Kashmera humbly asks for her blessings.

articles.title: Horoscope Tomorrow, April 28, 2026: Career Breakthrough Signs, Love Shifts & Money Warnings
articles.description: Horoscope tomorrow for 28th April 2026 brings a practical, high-focus day for all zodiac signs. Professional responsibilities, relationship communication, emotional balance, and money management become the top priorities. Several signs may feel mental pressure or confusion, making patience and clear thinking essential. At the same time, many opportunities for growth, recognition, and healing are available. Tomorrow’s horoscope shows that steady action, discipline, and mature choices can turn challenges into success.
